zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford於1274年4月1日在英國賀福爾克利福德受洗時，他的父親Roger II de Clifford31歲，母親Isabel Veteripont21歲。他於1295年11月12日與Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles在英國英格蘭賀福爾克利福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1296年登記服兵役。他於1299年被列為member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford)。他去世於1314年6月24日在蘇格蘭斯林史爾班諾可本享年40歲，埋葬在英國維斯特莫藍德沙普聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford于1274年4月1日在英国賀福爾克利福德受洗时，他的父亲Roger II de Clifford31岁，母亲Isabel Veteripont21岁。他于1295年11月12日与Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles在英国英格兰賀福爾克利福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1296年登记服兵役。他于1299年被列为member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford)。他去世于1314年6月24日在苏格兰斯林史爾班諾可本享年40岁，埋葬在英国維斯特莫藍德沙普聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford was christened on 1 April 1274, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, his father, Roger II de Clifford, was 31 and his mother, Isabel Veteripont, was 21. He married Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles on 12 November 1295, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He registered for military service in 1296. In 1299, his occupation is listed as member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford). He died on 24 June 1314, in Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Scotland, at the age of 40, and was buried in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford a été baptisé le 1 avril 1274, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger II de Clifford, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Isabel Veteripont, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles le 12 novembre 1295, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1296. En 1299, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford). Il est décédé le 24 juin 1314, à Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Angleterre.

de.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford am 1. April 1274 in Clifford, Herefordshire, England getauft wurde, war sein Vater Roger II de Clifford 31 und seine Mutter Isabel Veteripont 21. Er hat am 12. November 1295 in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1296 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1299 ist sein Beruf als member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford) aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. Juni 1314 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Scotland und wurde in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford è stato battezzato il 1 aprile 1274 nel Clifford, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Roger II de Clifford, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Veteripont, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles il 12 novembre 1295, nel Clifford, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1296. Nel 1299, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford). Morì il 24 giugno 1314, nel Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Scotland, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Cliffordが1274年4月1日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードで洗礼を受けた時，父Roger II de Cliffordは31歳，母Isabel Veteripontは21歳であった。1295年11月12日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードでMaud de Clare Baroness de Wellesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1296年に兵役登録をした。1299年，職業はmember of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford)と記録されている。彼は1314年6月24日にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアバノクブルンで40歳で死去し，イギリスウエストモーランドシャップスト マリー マグダリーネ'ス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford(이)가 1274년 4월 1일 영국 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger II de Clifford(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Veteripont(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1295년 11월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1296년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1299년, 그의 직업은 member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford)이었다. 그는 1314년 6월 24일 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 반옥k번에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford foi batizado em 1 de abril de 1274, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger II de Clifford, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Veteripont, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles em 12 de novembro de 1295, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1296. Em 1299, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford). Ele faleceu em 24 de junho de 1314, em Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Scotland, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford был крещен 1 апреля 1274 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger II de Clifford было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Isabel Veteripont, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles 12 ноября 1295 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. В 1296 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1299 годуработал по профессии member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford). Он умер 24 июня 1314 г. в местности банноккбурн, стирлингшир, Шотландия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford se bautizó el 1 de abril de 1274, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger II de Clifford, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Isabel Veteripont, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles el 12 de noviembre de 1295, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1296. En 1299, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament (whereby he is held to have become lord clifford). Murió el 24 de junio de 1314, en Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Scotland, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra.
